title: Dactylorhiza × guilhotii
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × guilhotii is a hybrid orchid species. It is a cross between Dactylorhiza elata and Dactylorhiza maculata. This hybrid is a result of interspecific hybridization within the genus Dactylorhiza. Dactylorhiza × guilhotii is native to the Pyrenees region of Europe. It is considered a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation.
uses: Dactylorhiza × guilhotii is a hybrid orchid species, and while its medicinal uses are not well-documented, there is some evidence to suggest that it may have properties similar to other Dactylorhiza species. Like its relatives, it may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, although this has not been extensively studied. Some traditional herbal remedies have used Dactylorhiza species to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems. However, further research is needed to confirm the specific medicinal uses and potential benefits of Dactylorhiza × guilhotii. More studies are required to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications.

intro: Dactylorhiza × guilhotii is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza sambucina and Dactylorhiza fuchsii, both of which are species of orchids commonly known as lady's slipper orchids. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × guilhotii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Dactylorhiza × guilhotii is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into syrups and lozenges to aid in the treatment of respiratory and throat-related issues.
